weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 13°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with amazing activities for your little one! From exploring recycled playgrounds to meeting designers at the Young V&A, there's so much to discover. Don't forget about the free family workshop at the Southbank Centre where you can stitch a giant collective artwork! With a mix of indoor and outdoor options, you can plan a fun-filled weekend, whatever the weather.

activities.title: Meet the Designer
activities.description: Open studio where you can meet a designer and spark your creativity.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on and interactive, perfect for a 3-year-old to engage with art and design.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: The museum is a short walk from the station.

activities.title: Family Activity: Micro to Macro!
activities.description: Drop-in family activity exploring the concept of Micro to Macro.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational, it encourages exploration and discovery.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for daily activity schedules.

activities.title: FREE EASTER FOOTBALL SESSIONS
activities.description: Free Easter football sessions for kids aged 6-16 years old.
activities.whyItWorks: While slightly above the age range, it's a great option for older siblings and promotes outdoor activity.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses and Overground to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check specific location for best route.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Ideal for toddlers with its safe and engaging design.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Located near Westfield and Olympic Park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Celebrates children's books, bringing them to life in an interactive setting.
activities.whyItWorks: The pop-up exhibits change seasonally, but are always well appointed and colourful.

transport.fromHackney: Tube, Overground and bus to Stratford.
transport.tip: Located close to Westfield and the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park with a full fortnight of free activities, sports and fishing.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers wide open spaces for running around.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for schedule.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life through fascinating exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers engaging displays and activities suitable for young children, sparking curiosity about everyday life.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hoxton.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for special family events.

activities.title: Nowruz Festival Family Day
activities.description: Crafts inspired by Nowruz and Persian culture. Haft-Sin Trail: Walk through Waterlow Park and find the ingredients for the Haft-Sin.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities and outdoor exploration make it perfect for a curious 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Highgate, then walk to Waterlow Park.
transport.tip: Pick up a booklet from the house during open hours.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: Explore a world of play and invention in a recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.
activities.whyItWorks: It is a limitless recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Walk across the bridge.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the latest times and ticket availability.

activities.title: Community Quilting: A Family Workshop
activities.description: Stitch a giant collective artwork at drop-in quilting workshops with textile artist Natasha Shah. The Clore Ballroom.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative, hands-on, and collaborative, making it a memorable experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Walk across the bridge.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the latest times and ticket availability.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a great hands-on experience for kids with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities and educational play zones.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to avoid queues, especially during weekends.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the wonders of the natural world, from dinosaurs to fascinating exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Best for curious minds, offers a thrilling mix of learning and fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaur exhibit and the interactive galleries.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Discover art and design from around the world.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers daily free activities and events for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Borrow a backpack of activities to help kids explore the museum.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum Easter Fun
activities.description: Easter-holiday family programme, including craft, themed activities, and Warhol-inspired art sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Maritime exhibits are hands-on, and Greenwich Park provides a spot to burn off energy.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: Pair it with a visit to Greenwich Park.

activities.title: Greenwich Park
activities.description: The oldest enclosed Royal Park in London and offers a fantastic free day out for families with kids.
activities.whyItWorks: The park offers wide open spaces for running around, a deer park to explore, and playgrounds for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: Head to One Tree Hill and take in sweeping views of the towering skyscrapers of Canary Wharf and London.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: London's highest public garden looks out over a 360° view of the city and it's a breath of fresh air that's absolutely free.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore the greenery, admire the landscaping and step out on to the open air terrace for that incredible overlook, all for zero money.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Liverpool Street Station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Booking in advance is essential!

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite.
activities.whyItWorks: Combining vibrant storytelling, engaging performances and interactive elements, theatre shows for kids in London are designed to captivate children's imaginations and introduce them to the world of theatre.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Tulip Festival
activities.price: Entry starts from around the mid-teens, with under-5s free
activities.description: Hampton Court Palace bursts into colour each spring with its annual Tulip Festival, showcasing more than 100,000 tulips across the palace gardens and courtyards.
activities.whyItWorks: Wander the Great Fountain Garden, the Knot Garden, and the Lower Orangery to discover vibrant beds, rare varieties, and unexpected displays tucked around the historic grounds. Daily Tulip Talks offer insights into their history and cultivation.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.price: Entry starts from around the mid-teens, with under-5s free
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun, outdoor activity with a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor adventure with animal encounters.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow, then bus to Hobbledown Heath.
transport.tip: Allow a full day to explore everything the park has to offer.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Children aged 8 and under are invited to discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve.
activities.whyItWorks: Great outdoor activity which combines animals with learning.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Walthamstow Wetlands.
transport.tip: Check the website for more details

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search high and low for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds. Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor adventure with animals, plus a fun code-breaking element.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station - Elephant Art Trail
activities.description: Battersea Power Station offers an elephant-themed art trail and a macaroni penguin 'egg' hunt on selected days.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun and engaging for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Battersea Power Station.
transport.tip: Check website for details.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Go behind the scenes of stop-motion animation and explore how Aardman's iconic characters and worlds are brought to life.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for kids who love Wallace and Gromit.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.
